summary: iter50 Cloudflare WAF IP Blocking — full backend + frontend validation. All 18 iter50 unit/integration tests PASS, plus the 22 regression tests across iter48 admin_security, iter49 security_alerts, and appeals continue to PASS (40/40 green). Frontend admin panel at /admin/security renders the new Cloudflare WAF · Blocklist section correctly: status pills 'Sin credenciales (CF_API_TOKEN + CF_ZONE_ID)' and 'Auto-block: desactivado' show as expected, the 'Bloquear IP' dialog opens, submitting 198.51.100.50 produces the warning toast 'Registro creado pero Cloudflare no confirmó: not_configured', the IP renders in the table with a red 'FAILED' badge, source='admin' shown, and clicking 'Desbloquear' (with confirm auto-accepted) removes the row (table returns to 'Ningún bloqueo registrado.' empty state). Backend startup logs verified clean: no errors related to cloudflare_ip_blocks index setup. Graceful degradation on missing CF creds behaves exactly as specified — records persist locally with status='failed' + reason='not_configured' so admins retain auditability.

issues: Radix DialogContent missing `Description` / `aria-describedby` — a11y warning in console (non-blocking, cosmetic).

action_items: (optional, cosmetic) Add DialogDescription or aria-describedby to the CF block Dialog to silence the Radix a11y warning.
critical_code_review_comments: cloudflare_blocks.create_block correctly enforces idempotency by short-circuiting on an existing ACTIVE record and never touching CF when the IP is already blocked. Good.
Startup handler wraps every ensure_indexes call in its own try/except so one collection's index failure cannot crash boot. Good pattern.

delete_block always transitions the local record to STATUS_DELETED even if the external CF call fails — this is intentional and correct for admin-facing consistency; the CF-side failure is logged (logger.error). Good.
The POST endpoint reuses `_require_admin_only` — role=admin is strictly required; employee gets 403 (verified in tests). Good.
cloudflare_client._is_configured is a pure env check and is reused by both is_auto_block_enabled and create_block. No hardcoded credentials. Good.
cf_deleting state is set but never surfaced in the UI (no per-row spinner) — non-blocking cosmetic.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (full happy path passed manually via Playwright)

test_credentials: Reused seeded users from /app/memory/test_credentials.md — no new credentials created. session_token cookie 'test_session_admin_X' used for the browser flow.
seed_data_creation: Test docs are self-cleaning: every iter50 test uses _cleanup_blocks to remove its own IP from cloudflare_ip_blocks before and after (or planted docs like 'manual-del-id' / 'pre-active-id'). Browser-run manual block on 198.51.100.50 was unblocked by the same test flow, so no lingering test data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter50 (Cloudflare WAF IP Blocking) is fully green — 40/40 backend tests + UI E2E happy path all pass with CF creds intentionally absent. Next iter can safely assume: (a) cloudflare_ip_blocks Mongo indexes are auto-created on startup, (b) all 3 admin endpoints (/api/admin/security/cloudflare/blocks GET/POST + DELETE) return proper 200/403/404, (c) frontend AdminSecurity.jsx exposes data-testids: cf-refresh-btn, cf-add-block-btn, cf-blocks-table, cf-block-row-{id}, cf-unblock-btn-{id}, cf-block-dialog, cf-block-ip-input, cf-block-notes-input, cf-block-submit-btn. When CF_API_TOKEN + CF_ZONE_ID are eventually set, the create_block path should transition status to 'active' with a cf_rule_id — worth adding a live-smoke test then.
